scopeNote: Prioritizes core high school subjects (grades 9-12) with seed DAGs and cross-subject links; designed to be expanded to 200-500 nodes per subject.

nodes.name: Pre-Algebra Fluency
nodes.description: Use integer operations, fractions, ratios, and proportional reasoning as bridge prerequisites for high school mathematics.

nodes.name: Linear Expressions and Equations
nodes.description: Model and solve one-variable and multi-step linear equations and inequalities.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Required for symbolic manipulation in kinematics.

nodes.name: Functions and Graph Interpretation
nodes.description: Interpret function notation, domain/range, transformations, and graphs in context.

crossLinks.subject: Computer Science/Technology
crossLinks.reason: Supports algorithmic modeling and input-output reasoning.

nodes.name: Systems of Equations
nodes.description: Solve linear systems graphically, substitution, and elimination; apply to word problems.

nodes.name: Polynomials and Factoring
nodes.description: Perform polynomial operations, factor expressions, and identify structure.

nodes.name: Quadratic Functions
nodes.description: Represent quadratics in standard/vertex/factored forms; solve and analyze roots and extrema.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Projectile motion models are quadratic.

nodes.name: Exponential and Logarithmic Models
nodes.description: Analyze growth/decay and inverse relationships with real-world modeling.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Population and biochemical process models use exponential/logarithmic forms.

nodes.name: Trigonometric Functions
nodes.description: Use unit-circle definitions, identities, and trigonometric equations in modeling.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Required for vectors and rotational systems.

nodes.name: Descriptive Statistics and Distributions
nodes.description: Summarize datasets with center/spread, normal models, and visualizations.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Economic indicators and polling analysis require statistical literacy.

nodes.name: Probability and Inference
nodes.description: Compute probabilities, sampling error, confidence intervals, and hypothesis tests.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Behavioral studies in psychology use inferential statistics.

nodes.name: Limits and Continuity
nodes.description: Evaluate limits graphically and analytically; define continuity and local behavior.

nodes.name: Derivatives and Applications
nodes.description: Differentiate elementary functions and apply rates of change, optimization, and motion.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Velocity and acceleration are derivative relationships.

nodes.name: Grammar and Syntax Foundations
nodes.description: Apply sentence structure, punctuation, and usage to improve clarity.

nodes.name: Close Reading of Informational Text
nodes.description: Annotate and interpret claims, evidence, and structure in nonfiction texts.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Historical document interpretation uses close reading.

nodes.name: Narrative and Analytical Writing
nodes.description: Write organized multi-paragraph responses with textual evidence.

nodes.name: Research Process and Source Evaluation
nodes.description: Develop research questions, evaluate source credibility, and avoid plagiarism.

crossLinks.subject: Computer Science/Technology
crossLinks.reason: Digital information literacy overlaps with online research evaluation.

nodes.name: Argumentative Writing
nodes.description: Construct thesis-driven arguments with warrants, evidence integration, and rebuttal.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Civic argumentation requires evidence-based claims.

nodes.name: Rhetorical Analysis
nodes.description: Analyze audience, purpose, style, and rhetorical strategies in complex texts.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Political speeches and founding texts require rhetorical analysis.

nodes.name: Synthesis Essay
nodes.description: Integrate multiple sources, including quantitative and visual evidence, into a coherent argument.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Interpreting charts and statistical summaries supports synthesis.

nodes.name: Literary Analysis Across Genres
nodes.description: Interpret themes, motifs, character development, and form in drama, poetry, and prose.

crossLinks.subject: Arts
crossLinks.reason: Script and performance interpretation are aligned.

nodes.name: Advanced Seminar Discussion
nodes.description: Lead evidence-based discussion, questioning, and collaborative interpretation.

nodes.name: Capstone Research Writing
nodes.description: Produce extended research papers with formal citation and revision cycles.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Scientific communication and evidence reporting overlap.

nodes.name: Biomolecules and Water Chemistry
nodes.description: Explain structure and function of macromolecules and water properties in living systems.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Chemistry
crossLinks.reason: Chemical bonding and polarity underpin biomolecule behavior.

nodes.name: Cell Structure and Transport
nodes.description: Model cell organelles, membrane transport, and homeostasis.

nodes.name: Cellular Energetics
nodes.description: Analyze photosynthesis and cellular respiration pathways.

nodes.name: Cell Cycle and Mitosis
nodes.description: Describe cell cycle regulation and mitotic division for growth and repair.

nodes.name: Meiosis and Mendelian Genetics
nodes.description: Predict inheritance patterns and connect meiosis to variation.

nodes.name: Molecular Genetics and Gene Expression
nodes.description: Explain DNA replication, transcription, translation, and regulation.

crossLinks.subject: Computer Science/Technology
crossLinks.reason: Bioinformatics uses data structures and algorithms.

nodes.name: Evolution by Natural Selection
nodes.description: Use evidence to explain adaptation, speciation, and common ancestry.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Scientific revolutions affect historical worldviews.

nodes.name: Ecology and Energy Flow
nodes.description: Model ecosystems, trophic levels, and nutrient cycles.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Earth & Environmental
crossLinks.reason: Biogeochemical cycles connect ecosystems and earth systems.

nodes.name: AP Biology Experimental Design
nodes.description: Design controlled investigations, analyze data, and justify claims with evidence.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Inference and uncertainty are required for biological conclusions.

nodes.name: Biology Capstone Communication
nodes.description: Communicate biological arguments in formal reports and presentations.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Research writing conventions transfer directly.

nodes.name: Atomic Structure and Periodicity
nodes.description: Relate subatomic structure to periodic trends and chemical behavior.

nodes.name: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Geometry
nodes.description: Compare ionic/covalent bonding and predict molecular shape and polarity.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Biomolecule behavior depends on polarity and bonding.

nodes.name: Stoichiometry
nodes.description: Use mole relationships to quantify reactants, products, and limiting reagents.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Proportional reasoning and equation balancing are prerequisite skills.

nodes.name: Thermochemistry
nodes.description: Analyze heat transfer, enthalpy changes, and calorimetry in reactions.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Energy conservation principles overlap.

nodes.name: Gas Laws and Kinetic Molecular Theory
nodes.description: Model gas behavior using pressure-volume-temperature relationships.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Function interpretation supports multivariable relationships.

nodes.name: Reaction Rates and Equilibrium
nodes.description: Explain dynamic equilibrium and factors affecting reaction rates and equilibrium position.

nodes.name: Acid-Base Chemistry
nodes.description: Apply pH, pKa, and neutralization concepts to aqueous systems.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Cellular processes are sensitive to pH.

nodes.name: Electrochemistry
nodes.description: Model oxidation-reduction and electrochemical cell behavior.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Electric potential and charge flow are connected concepts.

nodes.name: AP Chemistry Quantitative Labs
nodes.description: Conduct inquiry labs with uncertainty analysis, titration curves, and data interpretation.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Error analysis and inference support lab conclusions.

nodes.name: Materials and Applications
nodes.description: Connect chemistry principles to industrial, environmental, and biomedical contexts.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Earth & Environmental
crossLinks.reason: Pollution chemistry informs environmental mitigation.

nodes.name: Measurement, Units, and Vectors
nodes.description: Use SI units, dimensional analysis, and vector representations.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Vector components rely on trigonometric functions.

nodes.name: Kinematics in 1D and 2D
nodes.description: Describe motion using position, velocity, acceleration, and motion graphs.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Algebraic equation solving is required for motion problems.

nodes.name: Forces and Newton's Laws
nodes.description: Apply force diagrams and Newton's laws to predict translational motion.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Quadratic models appear in constant-acceleration contexts.

nodes.name: Work, Energy, and Power
nodes.description: Use energy conservation and work-energy relationships.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Chemistry
crossLinks.reason: Energy transfer is a shared core concept.

nodes.name: Momentum and Collisions
nodes.description: Analyze momentum conservation in isolated systems.

nodes.name: Rotational Motion and Torque
nodes.description: Apply rotational kinematics and dynamics, including torque and angular momentum.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Angular relationships require trig and periodic reasoning.

nodes.name: Oscillations and Waves
nodes.description: Model periodic motion, wave properties, and energy transport.

crossLinks.subject: Arts
crossLinks.reason: Sound wave properties underlie music acoustics.

nodes.name: Electricity and Circuits
nodes.description: Analyze electric fields, potential difference, current, and simple circuits.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Chemistry
crossLinks.reason: Electrochemistry and circuit potential are linked.

nodes.name: Fluids and Pressure
nodes.description: Apply fluid statics and dynamics including buoyancy and continuity.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Earth & Environmental
crossLinks.reason: Atmospheric and oceanic systems are fluid systems.

nodes.name: AP Physics Modeling and Labs
nodes.description: Develop and test physical models using inquiry labs and uncertainty analysis.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Advanced rates-of-change modeling strengthens physical interpretation.

nodes.name: Earth Systems and Spheres
nodes.description: Describe interactions among geosphere, hydrosphere, atmosphere, and biosphere.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Ecosystem dynamics depend on earth system interactions.

nodes.name: Weather and Climate Systems
nodes.description: Analyze atmospheric circulation, climate patterns, and energy balance.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Fluid and pressure models explain atmospheric behavior.

nodes.name: Geologic Time and Plate Tectonics
nodes.description: Use evidence to explain earth history, plate boundaries, and hazards.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Geography and resource distribution shape human settlement and conflict.

nodes.name: Human Population and Resource Use
nodes.description: Model population trends and resource consumption impacts.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Growth/decay models support demographic projections.

nodes.name: Energy Resources and Sustainability
nodes.description: Compare energy systems and evaluate sustainability tradeoffs.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Energy transformations and efficiency are core physics concepts.

nodes.name: Biogeochemical Cycles
nodes.description: Trace carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, and water cycles in natural and human systems.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Ecosystem productivity relies on nutrient cycling.

nodes.name: Pollution and Environmental Chemistry
nodes.description: Evaluate pollutant pathways, toxicity, and remediation strategies.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Chemistry
crossLinks.reason: Chemical mechanisms explain pollutant behavior.

nodes.name: Environmental Policy and Justice
nodes.description: Analyze policy frameworks, stakeholders, and environmental justice case studies.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Public policy analysis requires civic and governmental understanding.

nodes.name: Data-Driven Environmental Analysis
nodes.description: Use datasets and uncertainty estimates to make evidence-based environmental recommendations.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Inference and uncertainty quantification are required.

nodes.name: Local Environmental Action Project
nodes.description: Design and communicate an actionable local sustainability project with measurable outcomes.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Capstone communication and evidence-based writing are essential.

nodes.name: Historical Thinking Skills
nodes.description: Source, contextualize, corroborate, and construct historical claims.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Close reading is foundational for source analysis.

nodes.name: World History Foundations (1200-1750)
nodes.description: Trace state formation, trade networks, and cultural exchange in early modern world history.

nodes.name: Industrialization and Global Transformation
nodes.description: Analyze industrialization, imperialism, and social change from 1750 onward.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Earth & Environmental
crossLinks.reason: Industrialization links to population and resource impacts.

nodes.name: U.S. Foundations and Constitutional Development
nodes.description: Explain colonial contexts, revolution, constitutional design, and federalism.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Founding documents require rhetorical and textual analysis.

nodes.name: U.S. Expansion, Conflict, and Reform
nodes.description: Interpret causes and consequences of expansion, civil war, reconstruction, and reform eras.

nodes.name: Civic Institutions and Constitutional Principles
nodes.description: Analyze branches of government, checks and balances, rights, and liberties.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Argument writing supports constitutional and policy analysis.

nodes.name: Political Participation and Public Policy
nodes.description: Evaluate elections, interest groups, media, and policy-making processes.

crossLinks.subject: Computer Science/Technology
crossLinks.reason: Media literacy and information ecosystems affect civic participation.

nodes.name: Macroeconomic Indicators and Policy
nodes.description: Use GDP, inflation, unemployment, and fiscal/monetary policy models.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Interpreting time-series and distributions is essential.

nodes.name: Microeconomics and Market Systems
nodes.description: Analyze supply-demand, market structures, and consumer/producer behavior.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Function graphs and equilibrium models rely on algebraic reasoning.

nodes.name: Psychology Foundations (Elective)
nodes.description: Survey cognition, behavior, development, and research methods in psychology.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Neuroscience and behavior connect molecular and cognitive levels.

nodes.name: Novice Communication (Template)
nodes.description: Exchange basic information in interpersonal, interpretive, and presentational modes.

nodes.name: High-Frequency Vocabulary and Grammar
nodes.description: Use foundational vocabulary, agreement, tense markers, and basic sentence patterns.

nodes.name: Cultural Products, Practices, Perspectives
nodes.description: Interpret cultural contexts and compare with learners' own communities.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Historical context deepens cultural interpretation.

nodes.name: Intermediate Writing and Speaking
nodes.description: Narrate and describe in connected discourse across familiar topics.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Transfer of composition structure supports target-language writing.

nodes.name: Spanish Pathway: Intermediate High
nodes.description: Apply language functions and cultural competence aligned to Spanish high school progression.

nodes.name: French Pathway: Intermediate High
nodes.description: Build interpretive and presentational proficiency with francophone authentic texts and media.

nodes.name: German Pathway: Intermediate High
nodes.description: Develop communicative proficiency and intercultural comparison in German contexts.

nodes.name: Advanced Interpretive Communication
nodes.description: Analyze authentic audio/video/text sources on abstract and contemporary topics.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Rhetorical interpretation transfers across languages.

nodes.name: Advanced Presentational Writing/Speaking
nodes.description: Produce sustained arguments and explanations for academic and civic audiences.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Public issue analysis supports advanced presentational tasks.

nodes.name: AP Language and Culture Performance
nodes.description: Demonstrate college-level language proficiency through integrated skills and cultural analysis.

nodes.name: Visual Composition Foundations
nodes.description: Apply line, shape, contrast, and balance in 2D visual design.

nodes.name: Drawing and Observational Techniques
nodes.description: Use proportion, perspective, and value to represent form and space.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Perspective and spatial reasoning align with geometry/trigonometry.

nodes.name: Color Theory and Media
nodes.description: Manipulate hue, value, saturation, and material choices for visual effect.

nodes.name: Music Theory Basics
nodes.description: Read notation, intervals, rhythm, and harmony in short musical excerpts.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Rhythm patterns and wave periodicity connect to proportional reasoning.

nodes.name: Ensemble Performance Skills
nodes.description: Develop rehearsal techniques, listening, blend, and expressive performance.

nodes.name: Acoustics and Sound in Music
nodes.description: Relate pitch, timbre, resonance, and wave properties to musical performance.

crossLinks.subject: Physics
crossLinks.reason: Wave behavior governs musical acoustics.

nodes.name: Theater Foundations
nodes.description: Analyze scripts, blocking, characterization, and stagecraft fundamentals.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Drama interpretation and character analysis overlap.

nodes.name: Portfolio Development (Visual/Media)
nodes.description: Curate iterative work with reflection, critique, and revision evidence.

crossLinks.subject: Computer Science/Technology
crossLinks.reason: Digital creation and portfolio publishing tools are shared.

nodes.name: AP Music Theory Analysis
nodes.description: Perform harmonic analysis, part writing, and aural skills at AP depth.

nodes.name: Interdisciplinary Arts Capstone
nodes.description: Create and present an original interdisciplinary performance or exhibition with critical rationale.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Presentation, critique, and audience analysis are shared competencies.

nodes.name: Digital Citizenship and Safety
nodes.description: Practice secure, ethical, and responsible use of digital systems.

crossLinks.subject: Physical Education/Health
crossLinks.reason: Digital wellness and cyber safety overlap with health education.

nodes.name: Computing Systems Fundamentals
nodes.description: Explain hardware, software, operating systems, and data representation basics.

nodes.name: Algorithms and Pseudocode
nodes.description: Design and trace algorithms with sequencing, conditionals, and iteration.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Function thinking supports algorithmic decomposition.

nodes.name: Programming Fundamentals
nodes.description: Implement programs with variables, control flow, functions, and debugging.

nodes.name: Data and Analysis
nodes.description: Collect, clean, and visualize data; reason about patterns and limitations.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Statistical summaries and distributions are shared foundations.

nodes.name: Object-Oriented Programming
nodes.description: Model classes, objects, encapsulation, and modular design.

nodes.name: Data Structures and Efficiency
nodes.description: Use arrays/lists/maps and reason about algorithmic performance tradeoffs.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Biological datasets require structured storage and search.

nodes.name: Networks and Internet
nodes.description: Explain internet architecture, protocols, latency, and cybersecurity basics.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Information networks influence civic discourse.

nodes.name: Creative Computing and App Design
nodes.description: Develop user-centered digital artifacts with iteration and testing.

crossLinks.subject: Arts
crossLinks.reason: Digital media tools support portfolio creation.

nodes.name: Computing Impacts and Ethics
nodes.description: Evaluate societal, legal, and ethical impacts of computing systems.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Policy and rights frameworks govern technology use.

nodes.name: Movement Competency Foundations
nodes.description: Demonstrate core motor patterns and safe movement mechanics.

nodes.name: Fitness Principles and Goal Setting
nodes.description: Apply FITT principles and track measurable fitness goals.

crossLinks.subject: Mathematics
crossLinks.reason: Progress tracking uses descriptive statistics and trend analysis.

nodes.name: Nutrition and Energy Balance
nodes.description: Analyze nutrient roles, caloric balance, and dietary decision-making.

crossLinks.subject: Science - Biology
crossLinks.reason: Cellular energetics informs nutrition and metabolism.

nodes.name: Personal and Community Health
nodes.description: Evaluate determinants of health, prevention strategies, and public health systems.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Public policy affects community health outcomes.

nodes.name: Mental and Emotional Wellness
nodes.description: Practice stress management, self-regulation, and help-seeking strategies.

crossLinks.subject: Social Studies/History
crossLinks.reason: Psychology concepts support mental wellness literacy.

nodes.name: Team Strategy and Leadership
nodes.description: Apply tactical decision-making, communication, and leadership in team contexts.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Collaborative communication skills transfer across domains.

nodes.name: Lifetime Physical Activity Planning
nodes.description: Design sustainable personal activity plans for long-term health.

nodes.name: Digital Health and Media Literacy
nodes.description: Critique online health claims and manage technology habits for wellbeing.

crossLinks.subject: Computer Science/Technology
crossLinks.reason: Digital safety and responsible platform use are shared competencies.

nodes.name: First Aid and Risk Response
nodes.description: Apply basic emergency response, injury prevention, and safety protocols.

nodes.name: Health Advocacy Capstone
nodes.description: Design and present a school or community health initiative using data and communication strategies.

crossLinks.subject: English/Language Arts
crossLinks.reason: Capstone health advocacy requires formal evidence-based writing.
